scope.selection_note: Invoked from the gate-framework topic root to sequence the remaining children and the blockers discovered outside the topic. ILLUSTRATIVE FIXTURE: derived from a real manual analysis, but ids, timestamps, and digests are frozen synthetic values, not live board state.

drift_reasons.detail: t1147 (dirty in-flight conflict) archived on 2026-07-11; wave 1 is now partially satisfied.
A follow-up hardening task appeared for the risk gate after this trail was generated.

freshness.refresh_recommended_because: Wave 1's conflict-clearing is partly done and a new risk-gate follow-up exists; the remaining wave boundaries are likely still valid but entry membership is not.

narrative.problem_statement: The gate framework spans many children plus blockers that live outside the topic (a red Python suite, stale task premises, shared skill-file collision surfaces, missing model defaults). What order lands the framework safely?
narrative.recommendation_summary: Clear dirty/in-flight conflicts first so no wave lands on top of uncommitted foreign work. Then fix the red baseline — proving the engine against a failing suite proves nothing. Only then prove the orchestrator engine itself, land the procedure-backed gates that depend on it, and serialize the UI surfaces that would otherwise collide on shared skill files. Everything else is long tail and must not block.
narrative.method_note: Read the topic's open children, the gate registry, the current test-suite result, and board state. Baseline-suite redness was observed from an actual run; premise staleness was verified against current source, not the tasks' original claims.
caveats: Wave boundaries after wave 3 are advisory; the DAG only records hard dependencies inside wave 4.
The long-tail exclusions were reviewed once; new children created after generation are not covered.

waves.title: Clear dirty and in-flight conflicts
waves.purpose: Ensure no gate-framework change lands interleaved with uncommitted or half-merged foreign work on the same surfaces.
waves.why_now: Conflict-clearing is cheap now and exponentially messier after multiple waves have stacked on the dirty state.
waves.consequence_of_delay: Every later wave inherits merge ambiguity; bisecting a regression through interleaved foreign hunks becomes guesswork.

entries.rationale: In-flight work holding uncommitted changes on the gate materialization path; nothing in the framework can merge cleanly under it.
entries.expected_outcome: A clean, committed baseline on the gate materialization surface.
entries.why_order_matters: Landing engine work on top of its uncommitted hunks would force a manual disentangle later.

waves.title: Fix the red baseline
waves.purpose: Restore a green Python suite so subsequent engine verification is meaningful.
waves.why_now: A red suite makes every later 'gates pass' claim unfalsifiable — failures cannot be attributed.
waves.consequence_of_delay: Engine-proving in wave 3 would produce unattributable failures and false confidence.

entries.rationale: The Python suite is red for reasons unrelated to the gate engine; until it is green, gate verification results are noise.
entries.expected_outcome: Green baseline suite; gate runs become attributable.

waves.title: Prove the orchestrator engine
waves.purpose: Demonstrate the headless gate orchestrator end-to-end before anything depends on its verdicts.
waves.why_now: Procedure gates and UI surfaces in later waves consume engine verdicts; proving the engine late would invalidate their acceptance.

entries.rationale: The engine-proving core: dispatch, retry budgets, and ledger recording under real verifier processes.
entries.expected_outcome: A demonstrated engine run with recorded ledger entries for pass/fail/error paths.
entries.why_order_matters: Its verdict semantics are the contract every wave-4 gate implements against.
caveats: The task premise predates the ledger format change and was re-verified against current source before inclusion.

waves.title: Land procedure-backed gates
waves.purpose: Convert the remaining human procedures into checker gates on top of the proven engine.
waves.why_now: They hard-depend on engine semantics proven in wave 3.

entries.rationale: The derived active-gates tuple work; recorded dependency on the engine core makes its position a fact, not a preference.
entries.expected_outcome: Claim-time materialization of enforced gate sets.

entries.rationale: Risk-gate output format hardening; it consumes wave-3 engine semantics but has no recorded dependency — position is advisory.
entries.expected_outcome: Risk gate verifier robust to the H3 output format.

waves.title: Serialize conflicting UI surfaces
waves.purpose: Land the board/TUI gate surfaces one at a time because they edit the same skill and board files.
waves.why_now: After the gates exist, their UI can land; in parallel it would collide on shared files.
waves.consequence_of_delay: None functionally — but attempting these in parallel instead of serially produces repeated merge conflicts on skill sources.

entries.rationale: Gate-status board surface. Functionally independent, but it edits the same board action/binding region as the other in-flight board feature, so it is sequenced, not parallelized.
entries.expected_outcome: Board gate-status display landing in a conflict-free window.
entries.why_order_matters: Timing only; no functional ordering constraint.

observations.statement: The Python test suite is red for causes outside the gate topic. Any gate-engine acceptance run before it is fixed produces unattributable failures.
One engine child's description assumes the pre-change ledger format; its premise was re-verified against current source and the task is included with a caveat rather than taken at face value.
Two UI children and one non-topic board feature edit the same skill/board files; wave 5 exists to serialize them rather than to express any functional dependency.
The codeagent per-operation defaults config lacks entries for the new gate operations; without them, launches silently fall back to the heavy default model.

exclusions.reason: Agent/model + TUI split-out; valuable but consumes, rather than gates, the framework core.
Remote-mode variant; explicitly deferred behind the core landing.
Manual-verification remainder of a different effort; shares no files or dependencies with the gate framework.

evidence.summary: t1147 held Implementing with uncommitted changes on the gate materialization path.
Suite red with failures in modules unrelated to the gate engine.
Task text predates the ledger format change; premises re-verified against current source.
Risk-gate hardening follow-up; no recorded dependency on the engine core.
Key-files list overlaps the board bindings/action region touched by another in-flight feature.

evidence.ref: diff of codeagent_config.json defaults vs new operations
evidence.summary: No .defaults entries for the new gate operations in seed or live config.
